title: Xylobium wageneri
taxonomy: Xylobium wageneri is a species of orchid native to South America. It belongs to the subtribe Maxillariinae, within the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The genus Xylobium is part of the tribe Maxillarieae, which is a group of orchids that are terrestrial or epiphytic. Xylobium wageneri is further classified as a member of the family Orchidaceae, order Asparagales, and class Liliopsida. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics as a terrestrial orchid with funnel-shaped flowers.

intro: Xylobium wageneri, commonly known as the Wagener's orchid or Wagener's Xylobium, is a species of orchid that is native to Central and South America. The Wagener's orchid has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Xylobium wageneri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Xylobium wageneri is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
